title: oxytropis neglecta subsp. triflora
intro: Oxytropis neglecta subsp. triflora, also known as the triflora oxytropis, is a subspecies of the Oxytropis neglecta plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the pea family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Oxytropis neglecta subsp. triflora include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb for the treatment of various diseases. In herbal preparations, Oxytropis neglecta subsp. triflora is often used as a dried herb or in the form of extracts, and is traditionally consumed as a tea or added to medicinal formulations.
